Verified jw.nju.edu.cn and www.nju.edu.cn as Nanjing University-controlled domains by domain suffix and official site branding.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery located one primary Nanjing University Undergraduate School PDF guidance on undergraduate use of generative AI tools. The 2026 undergraduate thesis notice was treated as a duplicate workflow pointer to the same guidance and AI filing forms; the NJU news page was treated as event/news coverage rather than primary policy evidence.

stopConditions: Stop after primary official Undergraduate School PDF supports undergraduate-scoped generative-AI use claims.
Do not infer graduate, staff, research, or university-wide approved-tool rules from undergraduate guidance.
Do not use news coverage, search snippets, attachment labels, or duplicate thesis notices as claim evidence.
Do not stage raw PDF, HTML, screenshots, or full normalized text in Git.

citation.citationTitle: Nanjing University AI Policy Tracker record

artifacts.claimText: Nanjing University undergraduate guidance allows generative AI to assist with topic research, literature search, material organization, and reference formatting, but requires students to assess generated information and forbids directly copying generated content to replace independent research.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 关于本科生规范使用生成式人工智能工具的指导意见（试行）
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 第三条 基本原则。学校在本科教育教学中统筹考虑人才培养多元性、课程资源多样性等因素，据此探索在不同教育教学场景下，对生成式人工智能工具使用建章立制，并确保相关制度的可操作性与可衡量性。第四条 适用对象。本指导意见适用于所有本科生。
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Article 4 says the guidance applies to all undergraduate students.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: 第一章 总则，第四条
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: 关于本科生规范使用生成式人工智能工具的指导意见（试行）
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 关于本科生规范使用生成式人工智能工具的指导意见（试行）
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 第二章 允许使用范围及要求。在征得任课老师、指导教师书面签字（含电子签名）同意的前提下，可在以下范围内使用生成式人工智能工具：第五条 辅助收集整理资料。原则上，在选题调研、文献检索、资料整理、参考文献格式整理等不同阶段时，可借助生成式人工智能工具辅助完成。
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The allowed-use section is conditioned on written signed or electronically signed consent from the course instructor or supervisor.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: 第二章 允许使用范围及要求
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: 关于本科生规范使用生成式人工智能工具的指导意见（试行）
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 关于本科生规范使用生成式人工智能工具的指导意见（试行）
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 原则上，在选题调研、文献检索、资料整理、参考文献格式整理等不同阶段时，可借助生成式人工智能工具搜集信息，但须对生成式人工智能生成信息的真实性、准确性、可靠性进行辨识，不得直接抄袭搬运生成式人工智能工具生成的内容来替代自主研究。
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Article 5 allows AI-assisted information gathering and organization, but requires verification and bars directly copying generated content to replace independent research.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: 关于本科生规范使用生成式人工智能工具的指导意见（试行）
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 关于本科生规范使用生成式人工智能工具的指导意见（试行）
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 禁止使用生成式人工智能工具代写论文、作业、报告或作品中的关键内容，包括但不限于研究（实验）假设提出、选题意义、方案设计、创新性方法设计、研究（实验）数据、研究（实验）结果、分析与讨论、结论总结、直接生成学术引用与参考文献列表等。
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Article 8 prohibits using generative AI tools to write key content in papers, assignments, reports, or works.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: 关于本科生规范使用生成式人工智能工具的指导意见（试行）
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 关于本科生规范使用生成式人工智能工具的指导意见（试行）
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 在选择人工智能技术时，应使用经国家备案登记的服务工具；禁止滥用人工智能技术危害数据安全、侵犯知识产权、泄露隐私机密、传播有害歧视性内容或违反伦理道德规范；论文、报告等涉及保密内容的，除获得相关部门特别批准外，禁止使用任何生成式人工智能工具进行辅助处理，同时，严禁将文档中的任何数据、图片或其他敏感信息上传至人工智能平台。
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Article 12 addresses registered services, data security, privacy/confidentiality, and sensitive-information upload restrictions.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 关于本科生规范使用生成式人工智能工具的指导意见（试行）
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 如毕业论文（设计）需使用人工智能技术，学生应提前向指导老师与所在学院提交书面申请，说明使用工具名称、版本、参数设置、使用目的、使用环节及范围、使用方式及预期效果等，获批准后方可使用。
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Article 13 requires advance written application and approval before AI use in undergraduate thesis or project work.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 关于本科生规范使用生成式人工智能工具的指导意见（试行）
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 提交任务成果时，应专设人工智能工具使用声明页，说明使用的工具名称、版本、使用时间、使用过程、具体用途及对成果贡献影响，并亲笔签名，以使评审者能够准确评估学生自主创作与工具辅助界限。
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Article 15 requires a dedicated AI-use declaration page when submitting work.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 关于本科生规范使用生成式人工智能工具的指导意见（试行）
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 对于违反本规定使用生成式人工智能工具的行为，将视情节轻重给予相应的处理。包括：警告并责令改正；降低论文、课程或实验成绩；本科毕业论文（设计）视情节给予取消毕业论文（设计）答辩资格、取消合格成绩等处理；构成学术不端的，按照学校相关管理规定给予相应处分。
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Article 17 says violations may trigger warnings, grade impacts, thesis-defense consequences, or academic-misconduct discipline depending on severity.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow source-status claim because Article 4 directly states the guidance applies to all undergraduate students.
Approved because the allowed-use chapter directly conditions use on signed written or electronic consent.
Approved with scope limited to information collection and organization; the claim preserves the source's verification and no-substitution limits.
Approved because Article 8 directly prohibits AI substitution for key content in listed academic tasks.
Approved as an undergraduate guidance privacy claim; it does not infer a broader institutional data-governance policy.
Approved with undergraduate thesis/project scope only, backed by Article 13 advance-application text and Article 15 statement-page text.
Approved because Article 17 directly lists possible consequences for violating the guidance.

artifacts.title: Nanjing University staged undergraduate generative-AI guidance evidence
artifacts.summary: Staged evidence for Nanjing University uses one official Undergraduate School PDF hosted on jw.nju.edu.cn. Approved claims are limited to undergraduate generative-AI guidance: applicability to undergraduates, written consent for allowed use, allowed information-gathering support with verification and no substitution, prohibited key-content substitution, confidentiality and sensitive-information restrictions, thesis/project application and statement requirements, and possible penalties for violations. A 2026 thesis workflow notice and an NJU news page were rejected as duplicate or news-only sources for claim evidence.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Claims are scoped to Nanjing University undergraduate guidance and should not be read as graduate, staff, research, or university-wide approved-tool policy.
The PDF is a trial guidance document issued by the Undergraduate School on 2025-03-27.
The discovery pass did not find a separate graduate-wide or staff-wide NJU generative-AI policy source on official NJU-controlled domains.
The NJU news page and the 2026 undergraduate thesis notice were not used as substantive claim evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
